writeCommentTitle.description: Title for the page where a user can write a comment on a PREreview.

backToPrereview.description: Link that takes the user back to the PREreview page.

youCanWrite.message: You can write a comment on this PREreview of {preprint}.
youCanWrite.description: Text that appears alongside the title “Write a comment”.

beforeStartHeading.description: Heading for the section that contains information about what the user needs to know before starting a comment.

orcidLogIn.message: We will ask you to log in with your ORCID iD. If you don’t have an iD, you can create one.
orcidLogIn.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Before you start”.

whatIsOrcidHeading.description: Heading for the explanation of what an ORCID iD is.

whatIsOrcid.message: An <link>ORCID iD</link> is a unique identifier that distinguishes you from everyone with the same or similar name.
whatIsOrcid.description: Text following the heading “What is an ORCID iD?” explaining what an ORCID iD is.

reloadPageButton.description: Button that reloads the page so that the user can check if a task has completed.

carryOnMessage.message: As you’ve already started a comment on this PREreview of {preprint}, we’ll take you to the next step so you can carry on.
carryOnMessage.description: Message shown alongside the title “Write a comment” when the user has already started a comment of the PREreview.

enterYourCommentTitle.description: Title for the page where a user can write their comment on a PREreview.

errorEnterComment.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Write your comment” form without entering a comment.

whatNameTitle.message: What name would you like to use?
whatNameTitle.description: Label for the question about the name the user would like to use.

errorSelectName.message: Select the name that you would like to use
errorSelectName.description: Error message shown when the user tries submit the “What name would you like to use?” form without selecting a name.

whichNameTip.message: You can choose between the name on your ORCID record or your PREreview pseudonym.
whichNameTip.description: Information provided about the question “What name would you like to use?”.

whatIsPseudonym.description: Heading for the explanation of what a PREreview pseudonym is.

whatIsPseudonymDefinition.message: A <term>PREreview pseudonym</term> is an alternate name you can use instead of your real name. It is unique and combines a random color and animal. Your pseudonym is ‘{pseudonym}’.
whatIsPseudonymDefinition.description: Text following the “What is a PREreview pseudonym?” heading explaining what a PREreview pseudonym is.

whatIsPseudonymAccountability.message: Using your pseudonym, you can contribute to open preprint review without fearing retribution or judgment that may occur when using your real name. However, using a pseudonym retains an element of accountability.
whatIsPseudonymAccountability.description: Text following the “What is a PREreview pseudonym?” heading explaining why you might choose to use a PREreview pseudonym.

linkToOrcidId.message: We’ll link your comment to your ORCID iD.
linkToOrcidId.description: Text that accompanies the public persona choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.

linkToPseudonym.message: We’ll only link your comment to others that also use your pseudonym.
linkToPseudonym.description: Text that accompanies the pseudonym choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.

competingInterestsTitle.message: Do you have any competing interests?
competingInterestsTitle.description: Title for the page where a user can declare any competing interests they may have.

errorCompetingInterests.message: Select yes if you have any competing interests
errorCompetingInterests.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Do you have any competing interests?” form without selecting an option.

errorCompetingInterestsDetails.message: Enter details of your competing interests
errorCompetingInterestsDetails.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Do you have any competing interests?” form with a “yes” answer but without entering details.

competingInterestsTip.message: A competing interest is anything that could interfere with the objectivity of the comment on a PREreview.
competingInterestsTip.description: Information provided about the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

examplesCompetingInterests.description: Heading for the list of examples of competing interests.

competingInterestsRelationship.message: You have a personal relationship with the author of the preprint.
competingInterestsRelationship.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsCompetitor.message: You are a rival or competitor of the author of the preprint.
competingInterestsCompetitor.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsWorkedWith.message: You have recently worked with the author of the preprint.
competingInterestsWorkedWith.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsWorkPlace.message: You work at the same place the author of the preprint works.
competingInterestsWorkPlace.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsCollaborate.message: You collaborate with the author of the preprint.
competingInterestsCollaborate.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsPublishedWith.message: You have published with the author of the preprint in the last five years.
competingInterestsPublishedWith.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsGrantWith.message: You hold a grant with the author of the preprint or have other financial ties.
competingInterestsGrantWith.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsCommenter.message: Any of these same criteria hold true between you and another commenter who has already published a comment on this preprint.
competingInterestsCommenter.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsNo.description: Option for the “Do you have any competing interests?” question.

competingInterestsYes.description: Option for the “Do you have any competing interests?” question.

competingInterestsDetailsTitle.description: Label for the input for providing details having selected the “Yes” option for the “Do you have any competing interests?” question.

codeOfConductTitle.description: Title for the page where a user can confirm that they are following the Code of Conduct.

errorFollowingCodeOfConduct.message: Confirm that you are following the Code of Conduct
errorFollowingCodeOfConduct.description: Error message shown when a user tries to submit the “Code of Conduct” form without ticking the “I’m following the Code of Conduct” checkbox.

codeOfConductTip.message: As a member of our community, we expect you to abide by the <link>PREreview Code of Conduct</link>.
codeOfConductTip.description: Information provided for the “Code of Conduct” form.

examplesExpectedBehaviors.description: Heading for the list of examples of expected behaviors for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorLanguage.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorConstructive.message: Providing feedback that is constructive, i.e. useful, to the receiver.
expectedBehaviorConstructive.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorRespectful.message: Being respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences.
expectedBehaviorRespectful.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorGraceful.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorCommunity.message: Focusing on what is best for the community.
expectedBehaviorCommunity.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorEmpathy.message: Showing empathy towards other community members.
expectedBehaviorEmpathy.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

examplesUnacceptableBehaviors.description: Heading for the list of examples of unacceptable behaviors for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorAttacks.message: Trolling, insulting or derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks.
unacceptableBehaviorAttacks.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorUnconstructive.message: Providing unconstructive or disruptive feedback on PREreview.
unacceptableBehaviorUnconstructive.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorHarassment.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorConfidential.message: Publishing others’ confidential information, such as a physical or electronic address, without explicit permission.
unacceptableBehaviorConfidential.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorSexual.message: Use of sexualized language or imagery and unwelcome sexual attention or advances.
unacceptableBehaviorSexual.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorOther.message: Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting.
unacceptableBehaviorOther.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

followingCodeOfConduct.message: I’m following the Code of Conduct
followingCodeOfConduct.description: Label for the checkbox in the “Code of Conduct” form.

contactDetailsTitle.description: Title for the page where a user can provide their contact details.

errorEnterEmailAddress.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Contact details” form without entering an email address.

errorEnterValidEmailAddress.message: Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com
errorEnterValidEmailAddress.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Contact details” form with an invalid email address.

needToConfirmEmailAddress.message: We’re ready to publish your comment, but we need to confirm your email address first.
needToConfirmEmailAddress.description: Text that appears alongside the “Contact details” title explaining that the user needs to confirm their email address before they can publish their comment.

emailAddressUse.message: We’ll only use this to contact you about your account and what you publish.
emailAddressUse.description: Text that appears alongside the “Contact details” title explaining how the user’s email address will be used.

whatIsYourEmailAddressTitle.description: Label for the input field for the user’s email address on the “Contact details” form.

verifyEmailAddressTitle.description: Title for the page after the “Contact details” form.

howToVerifyEmailAddress.message: Please open the email we sent to {emailAddress} and follow the link. You’ll then be able to publish your comment.
howToVerifyEmailAddress.description: Text that appears alongside the title “Verify your email address” and explains what the user needs to do next.

onceEmailAddressVerified.message: Once your address is verified, you can close this page.
onceEmailAddressVerified.description: Text that appears alongside the title “Verify your email address” and informs the user that they can close the page once their email address is verified.

checkTitle.description: Title for the page where a user can check their comment before publishing.

checkYourDetailsHeading.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s details on the “Check your comment” page.

publishedNameHeading.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s published name on the “Check your comment” page.

changeName.description: Link for the user to change their published name on the “Check your comment” page.

competingInterestsHeading.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s competing interests on the “Check your comment” page.

changeCompetingInterests.description: Link for the user to change their competing interests on the “Check your comment” page.

noCompetingInterests.message: The author of this comment declares that they have no competing interests.
noCompetingInterests.description: Text that appears alongside the “Competing interests” heading when the user has declared no competing interests on the “Check your comment” page.

checkYourCommentHeading.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s comment on the “Check your comment” page.

changeComment.description: Link for the user to change their comment on the “Check your comment” page.

nowPublishHeading.description: Heading for the section to publish the comment after checking their answers.

nowPublishMessage.message: We will assign your comment a DOI (a permanent identifier) and make it publicly available under a <license>CC BY 4.0 license</license>.
nowPublishMessage.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Now publish your comment” explaining what will happen when the user publishes their comment.

publishingTitle.description: Title for the page that appears after the user clicks the “Publish comment” button.

publishingSeeShortlyMessage.message: You’ll be able to see your comment shortly.
publishingSeeShortlyMessage.description: Text that appears alongside the title “We’re publishing your comment” explaining that the user will be able to see their comment shortly.

publishedTitle.description: Title for the page that appears after the user’s comment has been published.

publishedYourDoi.description: Label for the DOI of the published comment.

publishedHappensNextHeading.description: Heading for the section that explains what will happen now the comment is published.

publishedSeeShortlyMessage.message: You’ll be able to see your comment shortly.
publishedSeeShortlyMessage.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “What happens next” explaining that the user will be able to see their comment shortly.
